title: Coryphopteris unidentata
taxonomy: Coryphopteris unidentata belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and the order Polypodiales. This plant is a type of fern, which is a vascular plant characterized by its leafy fronds and reproductive structures. At the kingdom level, Coryphopteris unidentata is classified as Plantae. The genus Coryphopteris is a group of ferns that are native to tropical Africa. Within the kingdom Plantae, Coryphopteris unidentata is also classified as a member of the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Coryphopteris unidentata, also known as the West African fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In some regions, Coryphopteris unidentata is used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant's leaves are sometimes used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to their potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coryphopteris unidentata for medicinal purposes.
